items.title: Wereldgoal Sano hoogtepunt van masterclass NEC in Alkmaar
items.summary: NEC heeft zaterdag indruk gemaakt door AZ in het eigen AFAS Stadion te overklassen. De ploeg van interim-trainer Leeroy Echteld was in grote delen van de Eredivisie-wedstrijd machteloos tegen de mannen van Dick Schreuder, die nu al elf wedstrijden ongeslagen zijn.

items.title: Zo overrompelt NEC de starre tactiek van AZ
items.summary: NEC heeft een eclatante zege geboekt bij concurrent AZ (1-3). In Alkmaar genieten de Nijmegenaren een groot tactisch voordeel, dat uitgebuit wordt door uitblinkers Kodai Sano en Basar Önal. Analyse van een risicovolle beslissing die niet lekker uitpakt tegen de vijfmansvoorhoede van Dick Schreuder.

items.title: VI Live: sterke start van Napoli, Hato heeft basisplaats bij Chelsea
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zaterdag 31 januari. Er staan drie duels in de Eredivisie op het programma, Liverpool krijgt bezoek van Newcastle United en de regerend kampioenen van Spanje, Duitsland en Italië komen in actie. Volg alle hoogtepunten in de VI Live.

items.title: Teleurstellend De Graafschap loopt ook in Helmond averij op
items.summary: De Graafschap heeft zaterdagmiddag opnieuw puntenverlies geleden in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. De Superboeren kwamen op bezoek bij Helmond Sport niet verder dan een doelpuntloos gelijkspel.

items.title: Arsenal thrash Leeds to reinstate 7-point PL lead
items.summary: Arsenal have reinstated their seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League after a 4-0 win against Leeds United.

items.title: Messi, CR7 jerseys headline Terry's $695K auction
items.summary: Former England and Chelsea captain John Terry netted $695,000 from a 50-item auction of his personal collection, headlined by match-worn Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo jerseys.

items.title: Wrexham boost PL promotion hopes with 1-0 win
items.summary: Wrexham moved back into the Championship play-off places after their hard- fought 1-0 win over Sheffield Wednesday at Hillsborough.

items.title: Arbeloa wants Madrid fan support after UCL blow
items.summary: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said he doesn't expect the team's fans to voice their disapproval as they return to LaLiga action on Sunday

items.title: Leeds 0-4 Arsenal, Brighton 1-1 Everton, Wolves 0-2 Bournemouth: clockwatch – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Updates from all of the Saturday 3pm (GMT) kick-offs ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Mail Emillia Arsenal fan Lenny Peters has messaged in to say: “As much as Arsenal win with ease against Kairat and Both Havertz & Gyokeres scored. They also seemed to get in each others space a few times. I wonder if they could be great if terrible together?” Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea v West Ham United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Premier League updates from the 5.30pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Email Billy A West Ham season-ticket holder has been banned for five games after the unfurling of a banner that said “Time 2 Sell – Name Your Price” in protest at the club’s board at last weekend’s home win over Sunderland. Joshua Wood, 27, was said to have breached regulations regarding items allowed inside the London Stadium, with the banner adjudged to have been too big. He plans to appeal. Wood said: “They’ve been clever in how they’ve approached it because they’re not saying I’ve been banned for protesting. They’re saying I’ve been banned for bringing up a banner which doesn’t fit the regulations of their dimension sizes. I have no doubt that if it said: ‘We love you, Brady and Sullivan’ we wouldn’t be talking today.” Brighton 1-1 Everton Leeds 0-4 Arsenal Wolves 0-2 Bournemouth Continue reading...

items.title: Bournemouth build on Liverpool win as Kroupi and Scott sink Wolves
items.summary: Two clubs in the process of a reset. If Wolves’ fate is all but sealed, they seek to carry good vibes into the Championship. Bournemouth’s objective was to make last week’s defeat of Liverpool the staging post for one of those streaks of good results that have made Andoni Iraola’s reputation. Mission accomplished for the Cherries, who could celebrate only their second away win of a hitherto troubled season, a first since August. In the performance of second-half sub Rayan, who supplied Alex Scott’s late clincher, there is much to look forward to. Without Antoine Semenyo, a scorer against Wolves last week. Iraola is finding solutions. A callow asset-stripped squad is still full of promise. The Basque paired Eli Junior Kroupi playing behind the centre-forward wiles of Evanilson. Kroupi duly delivered a brilliant first-half strike. Continue reading...

items.summary: null

items.title: Arsenal dankt onttroonde clubtopscorer 'Eigen Goal' en Madueke voor zege bij Leeds
items.summary: Arsenal heeft zaterdagmiddag geen fout gemaakt in de Premier League na een succesvolle Europese week. De koploper versloeg afgelopen woensdag Kairat Almaty in de Champions League, zaterdag ging Leeds United voor de bijl. Met dank aan ex-PSV'er Noni Madueke en wederom een eigen doelpunt.

items.title: Sixmilebridge sparkles in Scilly Isles success
items.summary: Sixmilebridge maintained his flawless chasing record to claim the Virgin Bet Scilly Isles Novices’ Chase at Sandown for Fergal O’Brien.

items.title: Garcia and Reed chasing Scotland's Hill at Bahrain Championship
items.summary: Patrick Reed and Sergio Garcia are four shots adrift of leader Calum Hill ahead of the final round of the Bahrain Championship.

items.title: Championship roundup: Dykes strikes as Charlton pile the pain on managerless Leicester
items.summary: Caleb Okoli sent off as Leicester lose at home 2-0 Southampton ease to 2-0 victory at Stoke Sonny Carey and Lyndon Dykes scored first-half goals for Charlton as they made it a 2-0 losing start for the Leicester interim manager, Andy King, at the King Power Stadium. The Leicester owner, Aiyawatt “Top” Srivaddhanaprabha, was in attendance for King’s first match in charge after the sacking of Martí Cifuentes on Sunday, but his side faced an uphill task from the moment the centre-back Caleb Okoli was dismissed in the 15th minute. Miles Leaburn looked set to race through on goal from Carey’s flick but Okoli pulled him back and the referee, Andrew Kitchen, showed him a red card. Continue reading...

items.title: The moment Saka got injured during Leeds warm-up
items.summary: Here is the moment that Bukayo Saka picked up an injury in the warm-up ahead of Arsenal's match with Leeds.

items.title: Germany rule out World Cup boycott despite calls to send Trump a message
items.summary: DFB rejects plea to make stand against US president ‘Our goal is to strengthen this force – not to prevent it’ Germany’s football federation, the DFB, has ruled out a boycott of the World Cup despite calls to send a message to Donald Trump. “We believe in the unifying power of sport and the global impact that a Fifa World Cup can have,” the DFB said. “Our goal is to strengthen this positive force – not to prevent it.” The federation said its executive committee met and discussed the option of a boycott of this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico, a consideration first proposed last week by the DFB vice-president, Oke Göttlich. Continue reading...

items.title: History-chasing Djokovic and Alcaraz meet in Australian Open final: Who wins?
items.summary: Novak Djokovic and that coveted Grand Slam No 25 is within touching distance, but he will have to overcome top-ranked Carlos Alcaraz, who is striving to become the youngest-ever man to complete a career Grand Slam.

items.title: What's meant for you won't pass you by - Calvert-Lewin
items.summary: Dominic Calvert-Lewin discusses the loneliness he experienced while waiting for a transfer this summer and how his form since joining Leeds has re-ignited his England hopes.
